channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Foreign Influence Targeting Alberta Separatism Raises Concerns, Report Finds
item.description: A recent report warns that both Russian and pro-Trump U.S. actors have been promoting Alberta separatism in an effort to undermine Canadian unity and increase public distrust in government institution...

item.title: Sierra Leone inaugurates industrial fishing port with Chinese collaboration
item.description: Sierra Leone has officially launched its first industrial fishing port at Black Johnson Village in the Western Area Rural District, marking a significant step in modernising the nation’s fisheries sec...

item.title: Heineken House Brings Sports, Music, and Hot Air Balloons to Lagos Fans
item.description: Lagos turn up on May 3 as Heineken kick-start 'A Day With Thibaut', the first edition of the Heineken House Experience. The event for true fans combine sports, music, and unique experiences for premiu...

item.title: Demonstrators obstruct entry to Russian pavilion during Venice Biennale
item.description: During the 2026 Venice Biennale, protest groups staged a demonstration that blocked public access to the Russian pavilion. The protests, which took place in a high-profile international cultural event...

item.title: La Salle edges closer to UAAP Season 88 championship after win over National U
item.description: In a decisive UAAP Season 88 women's volleyball match, the De La Salle Lady Spikers reinforced their dominance over the National University Lady Bulldogs. With this victory, La Salle moves a step clos...

item.title: West Bengal EC says no evidence of assault on Mamata Banerjee at vote counting centre
item.description: The Chief Electoral Officer of West Bengal, Manoj Kumar Agarwal, has dismissed the claims made by Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee that she was physically assaulted at a counting centre during the Assem...

item.title: Alberta Voter Database Breach Raises Safety Concerns for Domestic Violence Survivors
item.description: A recent leak of Alberta's voters list has raised serious safety concerns, particularly for domestic violence survivors. The breach exposed personal information, including home addresses, potentially ...

item.title: Senator Tony Nwoye Leaves ADC for NDC Over Party Crisis
item.description: For the latest development inside Nigeria Senate, Senator Tony Nwoye, wey dey represent Anambra North Senatorial District and also serve as Minority Whip, don officially dump the African Democratic Co...

item.title: Sen. Susan Collins Faces Democratic Challenger in High-Stakes Maine Senate Race
item.description: As she campaigns for a sixth term in the U.S. Senate, Republican Sen. Susan Collins of Maine remains a key target for Democrats, particularly Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer. Collins emphasizes t...

item.title: Aisha Yesufu Joins NDC, Announces Plan to Contest FCT Senatorial Seat
item.description: Activist Aisha Yesufu don waka from African Democratic Congress (ADC) go Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC). She announce say she wan run for FCT senatorial seat. For her X post, she yan say the next d...

item.title: Woman Reclaims Identity Documents After 15-Year Struggle with Family
item.description: Wanjiru Murage, a woman from Nairobi, has finally secured her passport and birth certificate after spending 15 long years facing hurdles from relatives who hid her late father's documents. After her f...

item.title: Jobless Father in Hluhluwe Sells Car and Cow to Fund Wedding
item.description: In Hluhluwe, northern KwaZulu-Natal, Mphakamise Nxumalo, a 42-year-old father of ten, recently married Ayanda Mdluli after selling his car and a cow to cover the wedding expenses. Nxumalo lost his job...

item.title: UKZN graduate celebrated for modest attire and touching moment with mother
item.description: At the University of KwaZulu-Natal’s recent graduation ceremony, 24-year-old Nokuthula Dlamini captured widespread attention for attending in a modest outfit rather than a new or 'classy' dress. Raise...

item.title: Nigeria’s New Ambassador to China Pledges Economic Boost for Kano
item.description: Nigeria don appoint Abdurrahman Bello Dambazau as ambassador go China, and e don promise say him go put Kano and Nigeria first for him new role. During him welcome reception for Kano, Dambazau talk sa...

item.title: Eric Rohman: The Swedish Poster Artist Who Defined a Century of Film Design
item.description: Eric Rohman, born in Nyköping, Sweden in 1891, became one of the few artists in the early 20th century to make a full-time living exclusively from creating posters. Over his prolific career, he produc...

item.title: Recreational Fishing in the US Represents a Major Source of Fish and Protein
item.description: New research reveals that recreational freshwater fishing in the United States captures significantly more fish than previously recorded. Scientists from the U.S. Geological Survey, the University of ...

item.title: Ontario Expands Transit Constables' Powers to Address Drug Use on Public Transit
item.description: Ontario's Progressive Conservative government has announced new powers for public transit special constables, allowing them to arrest or ticket individuals using illegal substances on transit and in t...

item.title: Senator Lacson Urges Preliminary Probe Into Top Officials Linked to Flood Control Scandal
item.description: Senator Panfilo Lacson has expressed the need for a preliminary investigation involving several high-profile officials, namely Martin Romualdez, Francis Escudero, Elpidio Villanueva, and Joseph Estrad...

item.title: Rudiger and Carreras Resolve Dressing Room Disagreement at Real Madrid
item.description: Real Madrid defender Antonio Rudiger don apologise to him teammate Alvaro Carreras after small wahala wey happen for dressing room following their 1-1 draw with Real Betis. E get as Daily Mail report ...

item.title: WA Government Raises First Home Buyer Stamp Duty Exemption Amid Rising Perth Property Prices
item.description: The Western Australian government has announced an increase in first home buyer stamp duty exemptions as the median house price in Perth surpasses $1 million. The exemption threshold has been lifted f...
